---
title: "Causal Exaggeration"
image: "images/balloon.jpg"
angle: 130
site: distill::distill_website
links:
  # - label: '`r fontawesome::fa("github")` GitHub'
  #   url: "https://github.com/vincentbagilet/causal_inflation"
  - label: 'Vincent Bagilet'
    url: "https://vincentbagilet.github.io/"
output:
  postcards::trestles
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE)

# Learn more about creating websites with Distill at:
# https://rstudio.github.io/distill/website.html

```

Hi and welcome!

This website gathers code and additional material for the paper [Causal Exaggeration](causal_exaggeration_paper.pdf).

In this paper I show that the use of causal identification strategies creates a trade-off between confounding and exaggerating true effect sizes due to a loss in statistical power. A previous version of this project was co-authored with Léo Zabrocki-Hallak.

A **new version**, with substantial additions, will be published soon. A preliminary version of the new introduction is available [here](causal_exaggeration_intro.pdf).
